mission.title: surrealdb: Feature: Support for storing large binary data / files
mission.description: Feature: Support for storing large binary data / files. Requested by 28+ users.

steps.title: Check current surrealdb setup
steps.description:
Verify your surrealdb version and configuration:
```bash
surreal version
```
This feature requires a working surrealdb installation.

steps.title: Review surrealdb configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant surrealdb configuration:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

In an application I am currently planning, I have a need to store files and in some cases whole directories with a wide variety of sizes (approx 1kB-1GB) together with structured data.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature: Support for storing large binary data / files
steps.description:
Thank you for submitting this pull request. We really appreciate you spending the time to work on SurrealDB. 🚀 🎉 

## What is the motivation?

See #1652. Files are a big part of data, and it's something we'd like to offer with a certain capacity in SurrealDB. This PR aims to be a first start in building an ecosystem of tools around files, whilst also building the foundation of features to come in the future.

## What does this change do?

This PR adds a couple new features and changes

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade surrealdb to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade surrealdb:
```bash
# Check current version
surreal version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/surrealdb/surrealdb
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Feature: Support for storing large binary data / files" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:

prerequisites.description: A working surrealdb installation or development environment.
